title: oxalis crispula
intro: Oxalis crispula, also known as the woodland sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and distinctive crinkled leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems due to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis crispula include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Oxalis crispula can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
